reason_thou:
1. Understanding the Issue:
The reproducer shows InstCombine changing a cttz call's second argument (is_zero_poison) from false to true: call noundef i32 @llvm.cttz.i32(i32 %y, i1 false) becomes call noundef i32 @llvm.cttz.i32(i32 %y, i1 true). Alive2 reports the transformation is incorrect: for %y = 0, the original returns 32 (bitwidth) which is a defined integer satisfying the noundef attribute; the transformed version produces poison (due to is_zero_poison=true and zero input), violating noundef and triggering UB. Thus InstCombine made the program less defined.

2. Analyzing opt's Log:
The first modification is in foldCttzCtlz(): setting operand 1 of cttz from false to true under the heuristic: "If ctlz/cttz is only used as a shift amount, set is_zero_poison to true." This matches the pattern: single use, operand1 == false, and single user is a shift (%res = shl i32 %x, %cttz). No other analysis influenced this initial change. A second visit then adds a range attribute. The unsoundness originates solely from the first change.

3. Root Cause Analysis:
The heuristic assumes it's safe to make the cttz/ctlz call produce poison for zero input because the only use is as a shift amount, and shifting by the original result (bitwidth) would produce poison anyway. However, that reasoning ignores call attributes: the intrinsic call is marked noundef, which forbids returning poison. Changing is_zero_poison to true introduces a possibility of poison when the input is zero, violating noundef even if the ultimate use would turn the value into poison later. The optimization lacks a guard against degrading definedness in presence of NoUndef. Correctness condition: we must not flip the flag unless (a) we can prove the input is non-zero (handled later by existing known-nonzero logic) or (b) the result is allowed to become poison (no noundef). Missing check for Attribute::NoUndef causes the bug.

4. Proposed Edit Point(s):
Edit lines 506-509 in InstCombineCalls.cpp (foldCttzCtlz). Add a guard to avoid applying this transformation when the return value has NoUndef (noundef) attribute. Rationale: prevents introducing poison where previously a defined integer was guaranteed, preserving semantic obligations of noundef. We rely on later code (lines 606-613) to still flip the flag when input proven non-zero; that remains sound even with noundef because zero case cannot occur.

Proposed change (conceptual):
Current:
  // If ctlz/cttz is only used as a shift amount, set is_zero_poison to true.
  if (II.hasOneUse() && match(Op1, m_Zero()) &&
      match(II.user_back(), m_Shift(m_Value(), m_Specific(&II))))
    return IC.replaceOperand(II, 1, IC.Builder.getTrue());

Patched:
  // If ctlz/cttz is only used as a shift amount, we can set is_zero_poison to true
  // because shifting by the bitwidth produces poison. However, do not do this if the
  // call's result is constrained by 'noundef' (NoUndef); that would introduce poison
  // for an otherwise defined zero input. The later known-nonzero logic will still
  // flip the flag safely when zero cannot occur.
  if (II.hasOneUse() && match(Op1, m_Zero()) &&
      match(II.user_back(), m_Shift(m_Value(), m_Specific(&II))) &&
      !II.hasRetAttr(Attribute::NoUndef))
    return IC.replaceOperand(II, 1, IC.Builder.getTrue());

(Implementation detail: Attribute::NoUndef corresponds to 'noundef'; using hasRetAttr is consistent with range check later.)

5. Conclusion:
The unsoundness arises from disregarding the noundef return attribute when converting cttz/ctlz to zero-poison form under a shift-only usage heuristic. Adding a guard to skip this transformation for NoUndef results preserves definedness and fixes the reported Alive2 counterexample while retaining the optimization for unconstrained calls or those proven non-zero. This targeted change addresses the root cause without impacting other foldCttzCtlz behaviors.
test_traj:
diff --git a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp
index 6cff3c7af91e..8fbfcb2aa89c 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp
@@ -504,8 +504,17 @@ static Instruction *foldCttzCtlz(IntrinsicInst &II, InstCombinerImpl &IC) {
   }
 
   // If ctlz/cttz is only used as a shift amount, set is_zero_poison to true.
+  // This is safe because shifting by the bitwidth is poison anyway. However,
+  // do not apply this fold if the intrinsic's return value is marked 'noundef'
+  // because flipping the flag would allow the intrinsic to produce poison for
+  // a zero input (when is_zero_poison=true) where previously it was guaranteed
+  // to return a defined integer equal to the bitwidth. That would violate the
+  // NoUndef contract and make the program less defined. If we can later prove
+  // the input is non-zero, the fold below guarded by known-nonzero logic will
+  // still canonicalize the flag safely.
   if (II.hasOneUse() && match(Op1, m_Zero()) &&
-      match(II.user_back(), m_Shift(m_Value(), m_Specific(&II))))
+      match(II.user_back(), m_Shift(m_Value(), m_Specific(&II))) &&
+      !II.hasRetAttr(Attribute::NoUndef))
     return IC.replaceOperand(II, 1, IC.Builder.getTrue());
 
   Constant *C;

+  // the input is non-zero, the fold below guarded by known-nonzero logic will
+  // still canonicalize the flag safely.
   if (II.hasOneUse() && match(Op1, m_Zero()) &&
-      match(II.user_back(), m_Shift(m_Value(), m_Specific(&II))))
+      match(II.user_back(), m_Shift(m_Value(), m_Specific(&II))) &&
+      !II.hasRetAttr(Attribute::NoUndef))
     return IC.replaceOperand(II, 1, IC.Builder.getTrue());
 
   Constant *C;

@@ -504,8 +504,17 @@ static Instruction *foldCttzCtlz(IntrinsicInst &II, InstCombinerImpl &IC) {
   }
 
   // If ctlz/cttz is only used as a shift amount, set is_zero_poison to true.
+  // This is safe because shifting by the bitwidth is poison anyway. However,
+  // do not apply this fold if the intrinsic's return value is marked 'noundef'
+  // because flipping the flag would allow the intrinsic to produce poison for
+  // a zero input (when is_zero_poison=true) where previously it was guaranteed
+  // to return a defined integer equal to the bitwidth. That would violate the
+  // NoUndef contract and make the program less defined. If we can later prove

   if (II.hasOneUse() && match(Op1, m_Zero()) &&
-      match(II.user_back(), m_Shift(m_Value(), m_Specific(&II))))
+      match(II.user_back(), m_Shift(m_Value(), m_Specific(&II))) &&

     return IC.replaceOperand(II, 1, IC.Builder.getTrue());

diff --git a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp
index 6cff3c7af91e..b67e52226fa4 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp
@@ -504,9 +504,16 @@ static Instruction *foldCttzCtlz(IntrinsicInst &II, InstCombinerImpl &IC) {
   }
 
   // If ctlz/cttz is only used as a shift amount, set is_zero_poison to true.
+  // If the intrinsic is marked 'noundef', we must drop that attribute because
+  // setting is_zero_poison=true allows the result to become poison when the
+  // input is zero. (It is still safe to keep 'noundef' when we later prove the
+  // input is non-zero; see the known-nonzero fold below.)
   if (II.hasOneUse() && match(Op1, m_Zero()) &&
-      match(II.user_back(), m_Shift(m_Value(), m_Specific(&II))))
+      match(II.user_back(), m_Shift(m_Value(), m_Specific(&II)))) {
+    if (II.hasRetAttr(Attribute::NoUndef))
+      II.removeRetAttr(Attribute::NoUndef);
     return IC.replaceOperand(II, 1, IC.Builder.getTrue());
+  }
 
   Constant *C;
